text.title: BackgroundService + Channel<T> Queue Consumer
text.summary: Producer/consumer pattern using a bounded Channel<T> and BackgroundService with per-item scoped DI resolution.

rule.statement: Always use a bounded Channel<T> with an explicit FullMode; never use Channel.CreateUnbounded.
rule.rationale: Unbounded channels build up memory without limit under load. Explicit capacity + FullMode (Wait, DropOldest, or DropWrite) forces the producer to handle backpressure instead of silently growing the heap.

section.title: BackgroundService implementation

rule.statement: Resolve scoped services inside ExecuteAsync via IServiceProvider.CreateScope() per item; never inject scoped services directly into the singleton BackgroundService.
rule.rationale: BackgroundService is registered as a singleton. Injecting a scoped service (DbContext, repository) directly causes a scope-mismatch exception at runtime on first access.

rule.statement: Wrap per-item processing in try/catch inside the ReadAllAsync loop; rethrow OperationCanceledException only when stoppingToken is cancelled.
rule.rationale: An unhandled exception inside ExecuteAsync terminates the hosted service silently in .NET 6+ (the host logs but keeps running). One bad item must not kill the consumer loop.

example.note: ReadAllAsync exits cleanly when the channel is completed or the token is cancelled — no manual while loop needed.

section.reference: Full skeleton with all required pieces wired together
Program.cs registration for channel + queue + hosted service
